title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues on March 6, 2026, as part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour.'
Welles, an Arkansas native, brought political commentary through music, covering themes from war to social issues.
The concert featured 20 songs including original work and covers of Black Sabbath, Radiohead, and John Denver.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Shohei Ohtani and Seiya Suzuki also homered for Japan, contributing to a 5-3 lead in the third inning.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game at Tokyo Dome during his visit to Japan with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, from Roslindale, will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026 supporting Mass. Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk aims to raise funds for Mass. Eye and Ear, where she has been treated for nearly 10 years.
She educates in Boston Public Schools and fulfills her dream to participate in the marathon for a personal cause.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler joins ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team alongside Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler, ESPN's longest-serving NBA analyst, began his role as color analyst for NBA Finals in August 2025.
Legler praises Celtics' performance, highlighting Joe Mazzulla's coaching and Jaylen Brown's All-NBA potential.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after a 298-day recovery, scoring 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists.
Maine native Cooper Flagg made his TD Garden debut, achieving 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists despite a foot injury.
Mavericks' Flagg received significant fan support in Boston, highlighting his connection to Celtics' star Jayson Tatum as a mentor.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned after 298 days, helping the Celtics beat the Mavericks with 15 points, 12 rebounds, 7 assists.
Charles Barkley impressed by Tatum's return but maintains Cavaliers as Eastern Conference favorites after acquiring James Harden.
Barkley criticized the notion Tatum should have sat out the season, praising his decision to play once healthy.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers, of Pittsfield, was arrested for impersonating a U.S. Army veteran to obtain government benefits since the 1990s.
Sommers allegedly used a stolen identity to collect Social Security and VA benefits, totaling thousands of dollars.
He faces charges including making false statements and numerous offenses, with potential penalties up to five years imprisonment.

title: Watch: Body cam video shows officer rescuing man, 78, from burning truck
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Easton police officer Dean Soucie rescued a 78-year-old man from a burning pickup after a crash on Turnpike Street.
The crash involved three vehicles, including a 1993 Mack dump truck and a 2013 Ford F-350 that trapped the driver.
The rescued driver, Francis Leverone, suffered a dislocated hip and was hospitalized with serious, non-life-threatening injuries.

title: US attorney going after money forfeited by Mass. resident in romance crypto fraud scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Federal prosecutors seek to recover over $327,000 in cryptocurrency linked to a romance scam in Massachusetts.
The scheme involved a fake online relationship leading the victim to invest in crypto via wallets controlled by scammers.
Authorities seized unhosted digital wallets in August 2025, aiming to permanently take control of laundered Tether funds.

title: Jayson Tatum’s fashion also makes a return in his comeback from Achilles tear
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum showcased a stylish mix of Parisian luxury and '90s nostalgia before his Celtics game.
Tatum returned from an Achilles tear with a solid performance, recording 27 minutes and a double-double.
His pregame fashion included a mix of Lanvin with Timberlands, drawing widespread attention and admiration.
